standard_description: Recognize and correct vague pronouns (i.e., ones with unclear or ambiguous antecedents).
subject: Language

khan_exercises.title: Subject-verb agreement
khan_exercises.description: Agreement is the art of making sure that sentence parts connect with one another.

items.question_content:
**Select the correct form of the verb to fill in the blank.**

>In England, people \_\_\_\_\_ lots of tea.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Subjects and verbs must agree in number. Singular subjects require singular verbs, and plural subjects require plural verbs.
 
* The dog **barks**.
* The dogs **bark**.
"People" is a plural subject, so we should use the plural verb **"drink"**.

"Door" is a singular subject, so we should use the singular verb **"was"**.

khan_exercises.title: Pronoun-antecedent agreement
khan_exercises.description: Pronouns and their antecedents need to agree in number.

items.question_content:
**Choose the option where the pronouns and their antecedents agree in number.**

>The Johnsons bought \_\_\_\_\_\_  first house when they moved to the suburbs.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Pronouns **must agree** with the nouns they represent (their *antecedents*). Singular nouns need singular pronouns, and plural nouns need plural pronouns:

* The dog wagged **its** tail.
* The dogs wagged **their** tails.

Find the noun that the pronoun refers to, and make sure they match!
"The Johnsons" is a plural noun, so we should use the plural pronoun **"their"**.

items.question_content:
**Choose the option where the pronouns and their antecedents agree in number.**

>I love _The Hobbit_; \_\_\_\_\_\_  is my favorite book.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Pronouns **must agree** with the nouns they represent (their *antecedents*). Singular nouns need singular pronouns, and plural nouns need plural pronouns:

* The dog wagged **its** tail.
* The dogs wagged **their** tails.

Find the noun that the pronoun refers to, and make sure they match!
"*The Hobbit*" is a singular noun, so we should use the singular pronoun **"it"**.

items.question_content:
**Choose the option where the pronouns and their antecedents agree in number.**

> Miran spent three hours shopping for a mattress because \_\_\_\_\_\_  wanted to find a good one.


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Pronouns **must agree** with the nouns they represent (their *antecedents*). Singular nouns need singular pronouns, and plural nouns need plural pronouns:

* The dog wagged **its** tail.
* The dogs wagged **their** tails.

Find the noun that the pronoun refers to, and make sure they match!
"Miran" is the name of a person, so we should use the personal pronoun **"she"**.
